{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",

  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Names the load-bearing concept (physics-informed neural network), shows where the draft drifts from it (training objective is MSE instead of PDE residual), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.4": "Maps against the nearest 3 works (SIREN, DeepONet) and identifies sharp vs rhetorical delta.",
    "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (activation choice is the principal driver of accuracy).",
    "RX.3": "Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (generalization claim is unsupported).",
    "RX.5": "Major rewrites are traced; minor edits are not."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.8,
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 0.6,

  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: Names the load-bearing concept (physics-informed neural network), shows where the draft drifts from it (training objective is MSE instead of PDE residual), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.4: Maps against the nearest 3 works (SIREN, DeepONet) and identifies sharp vs rhetorical delta.
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (activation choice is the principal driver of accuracy).
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (generalization claim is unsupported).
rationale.RX.5: Major rewrites are traced; minor edits are not.
